author: Dr. Linda Wong
department: Family Medicine — Johns Hopkins Community Health Partnership
content:
NEW PATIENT INTAKE — 8/22/2018, 10:00 AM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (DOB 8/12/2003, Age 15)
Insurance: Maryland Medicaid (newly enrolled following immigration)
Interpreter: Spanish (in-person, MA-translated)

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Establish primary care. Required immigration physical and immunization catch-up.

HPI:
Ms. Sandoval is a 15-year-old female who immigrated from rural Guatemala with her mother and younger brother approximately 11 months ago. Sponsored entry; living with maternal aunt in East Baltimore. Per mother, she has been generally healthy. No chronic conditions known. Mother reports patient had several episodes of "low blood" as a younger child in Guatemala, treated with iron supplementation. Otherwise no major illnesses. No surgeries. No hospitalizations. Mother brings limited records, mostly immunization card.

Patient herself reports no current symptoms — no fevers, weight loss, fatigue, GI, GU, MSK, skin, or neuro complaints. Doing well in 10th grade ESL program. Working occasional weekends helping her aunt clean.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS: All negative.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: "Low blood" / anemia in childhood per mother, treated with iron, resolved.

MEDICATIONS: None.
ALLERGIES: NKDA.

FAMILY HISTORY: Maternal aunt with SLE — "lupus" — diagnosed in her 20s in Guatemala, disabled. Mother with hypothyroidism. Maternal grandmother with diabetes. Father deceased age 39 (motor vehicle accident); per mother, he had treated TB years before his death.

SOCIAL HISTORY: Recent immigrant from rural Guatemala (Quiché department). Lives with mother, brother (age 9), maternal aunt. Attends Patterson High School ESL program. Speaks Spanish primarily; learning English. No tobacco, alcohol, drug use. Not sexually active. Diet variable — household food insecurity at times.

VITALS:
BP 108/66, HR 78, RR 14, T 98.2 F, SpO2 100% RA. Weight 49.0 kg. Height 149 cm. BMI 22.1.

EXAM:
General: Well-appearing adolescent female, no acute distress.
HEENT: Normal. Oropharynx clear. No LAD. Thyroid normal.
CV: RRR no murmur.
Lungs: Clear.
Abdomen: Soft, NT/ND.
MSK: Normal range of motion, no joint swelling or tenderness.
Skin: Normal, no rash.
Neuro: Intact.

LABS (today):
- CBC: WBC 5.2, Hgb 10.8 (low; MCV 76, hypochromic microcytic), Plt 280
- Iron studies: Iron 32 (low), TIBC 410 (high), Ferritin 6 (low) — IRON DEFICIENT
- CMP normal
- Hepatitis B sAg negative, HBV core Ab negative, HBV sAb positive (vaccinated)
- Hepatitis C Ab negative
- HIV 1/2 Ag/Ab non-reactive
- QuantiFERON-TB Gold: indeterminate; will repeat or do PPD
- UA normal
- Pregnancy test (urine HCG): negative

IMMUNIZATIONS RECORDED FROM MEXICAN IMMUNIZATION CARD: BCG (infancy), MMR x 2, DPT x 5, polio x 4, varicella (unclear).
ADDITIONAL IMMUNIZATIONS GIVEN TODAY: Tdap, HPV #1, MCV4.

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
1. Iron-deficiency anemia. Ferrous sulfate 325 mg PO daily x 3 months. Repeat CBC + iron studies in 3 months.
2. TB screening — CXR ordered for indeterminate IGRA. RTC if PPD reactive.
3. Immigration catch-up immunizations — completed today; series to continue (HPV #2 in 2 months).
4. Establish PCP follow-up annually or PRN.
5. Spanish-language anticipatory guidance given (nutrition, school, mental health resources). Mother understood.

Visit time: 60 min (interpreter time 35 min).
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Linda Wong, MD
Johns Hopkins Community Health Partnership
Date: 8/22/2018
